publicSideBand: The city maintains the utility-owned portion to the meter and verifies eligible lead service line work under its lead reduction program
privateSideBand: Owners hire the contractor and the city offers cost-share reimbursement for eligible lead service line replacement work
fullReplacementBand: Service line repairs generally cost thousands of dollars and can reach tens of thousands in complex locations while city reimbursement reduces owner cost in eligible cases
housingTypeAssumption: Iowa City publishes reimbursement and broad cost language instead of contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on lead or suspected lead service status city verification and reimbursement paperwork
restorationAssumption: Permits contractor coordination and restoration vary by site and remain owner-managed before reimbursement review
methodologySummary: Iowa City does not publish a fixed local replacement price and instead explains that service line repairs can range from thousands to tens of thousands while the city reimburses part of eligible owner-managed work.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners remain responsible for hiring the contractor and telling the contractor about the city's cost-share program before work begins so reimbursement can be reviewed after the city verifies eligibility.
